synthesis.summary: MRNA surged 14.25% on more than double its average volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The catalyst could be options flow, short covering (MRNA has historically carried high short interest), sector rotation into biotech, or an untracked catalyst such as a conference presentation or rumor — none of which are evidenced in the available news.

XYZ surged 7.4% after reporting a blowout Q1 driven by Cash App outperformance, with management raising full-year guidance that analysts interpret as signaling continued improvement into 2026. The combination of an earnings beat and upward guidance revision directly accounts for both the magnitude and elevated volume (1.7x relative).

CALY is up ~19% on nearly double its average volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The RSI at 72 and bullish MACD cross suggest momentum-driven buying, but the catalyst — whether options flow, short squeeze, or undiscovered news — is not visible here.

CAI dropped 16.47% on nearly 4x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The sell-off could reflect options activity, institutional repositioning, or information not captured in public headlines, but nothing here supports a fundamental catalyst.

DraftKings beat both revenue and earnings estimates in Q1 2026, directly driving the 2.32% gain today and the broader 12.31% 5-day run as the market prices in stronger-than-expected business momentum. Volume running at 2.2x normal confirms institutional reaction to the earnings print.
